case_title: Inspect Low Battery Warning feature for ASPICE compliance gaps

feature.name: Low Battery Warning
feature.description: The rider shall be warned when the vehicle battery state of charge is low.

input_evidence.text: When the vehicle battery state of charge becomes low, the rider shall be warned on the display.
BatteryWarningController activates LOW_BATTERY_TELLTALE when batterySocPercent < configuredLowBatteryThreshold.

requirements.statement: The rider shall be warned when the vehicle battery state of charge is low.
The system shall warn the rider when the battery is low.
The display software shall activate LOW_BATTERY_TELLTALE when batterySocPercent is below configuredLowBatteryThreshold.
requirements.verification_criteria: Set batterySocPercent below configuredLowBatteryThreshold and verify that LOW_BATTERY_TELLTALE is activated.

expected_findings.message: SYS-REQ-BAT-001 has no linked SYS.5 test case.
SYS.2 says 'battery is low', while SWE.1 uses configuredLowBatteryThreshold. SYS.2 should define or reference the threshold.

case_title: Inspect Settings Menu Navigation feature for ASPICE compliance gaps

feature.name: Settings Menu Navigation
feature.description: The rider can open and navigate the Settings menu from the main display screen.

input_evidence.text: The rider can open the Settings menu from the main display screen. The menu shall allow navigation between Display, Connectivity, and Vehicle Info sections.
MenuRouter maps SETTINGS_ROOT to DISPLAY_SETTINGS, CONNECTIVITY_SETTINGS, and VEHICLE_INFO.

requirements.statement: The rider shall be able to access and navigate the Settings menu from the main display screen.
The system shall provide a Settings menu accessible from the main display screen with Display, Connectivity, and Vehicle Info sections.
requirements.verification_criteria: Open the Settings menu from the main display screen and verify that Display, Connectivity, and Vehicle Info sections are available.

requirements.statement: The menu routing software shall map the Settings root menu to Display Settings, Connectivity Settings, and Vehicle Info menu states.
requirements.verification_criteria: Verify that menu routing maps the Settings root to all required submenu states.

test_cases.title: Verify Settings menu sections
test_steps: Verify that Display, Connectivity, and Vehicle Info sections are available.
test_cases.expected_result: The Settings menu contains Display, Connectivity, and Vehicle Info sections.

expected_findings.message: SWE-REQ-MENU-001 is not linked to an upstream SYS.2 requirement.
SYS-REQ-MENU-001 is linked to a test case but has no downstream SWE.1 allocation link.

case_title: Inspect Display Communication Fault Handling feature for ASPICE compliance gaps

feature.name: Display Communication Fault Handling
feature.description: The rider is informed when display communication is lost and stale critical values are not shown as valid.

input_evidence.text: If a display communication fault is detected, the rider shall be informed by a warning state. The system shall avoid showing stale critical values as valid values.
DisplayComMonitor reports DISPLAY_COM_LOSS when no valid display communication frame is received within the configured timeout. The UI enters degraded mode.

requirements.statement: The rider shall be informed when a display communication fault prevents reliable presentation of information.
The system shall detect display communication loss.
requirements.verification_criteria: Disconnect display communication and verify that communication loss is detected.

requirements.statement: The system shall enter a display warning or degraded state when display communication is lost for longer than the configured timeout and shall prevent stale critical values from being presented as valid.
requirements.verification_criteria: Inject display communication loss longer than the configured timeout and verify that the system enters the warning or degraded state and does not present stale critical values as valid.

requirements.statement: The display communication monitoring software shall detect missing valid display communication frames within the configured timeout and request degraded UI mode.
requirements.verification_criteria: Simulate missing display frames and verify that degraded UI mode is requested after the configured timeout.

test_cases.title: Verify display degraded mode on communication loss
test_steps: Start the system with valid display communication.
Interrupt display communication longer than the configured timeout.
Observe the display state and critical value presentation.
test_cases.expected_result: The system enters display warning or degraded state and stale critical values are not presented as valid.

expected_findings.message: SYS-REQ-DIAG-001 is safety-relevant but is not reviewed.
SYS-REQ-DIAG-001 detects display communication loss but does not preserve stakeholder intent about warning the rider and avoiding stale critical values.
SYS-REQ-DIAG-001 has verification criteria but no linked SYS.5 test case.

case_title: Inspect Service Mode Indicator feature for ASPICE compliance gaps

feature.name: Service Mode Indicator
feature.description: The display shows a service mode indicator when service mode is active.

input_evidence.text: The display should probably show a special service mode indicator.

requirements.statement: The system shall display a service mode indicator when service mode is active.

expected_findings.message: SYS-REQ-SERVICE-001 has no upstream stakeholder requirement or approved source traceability.
SYS-REQ-SERVICE-001 has no downstream traceability to SWE.1, architecture, or verification artifacts.
SYS-REQ-SERVICE-001 has no linked SYS.5 test case.

case_title: Inspect clean reference feature with no expected compliance gaps

feature.name: Reference Good Requirement Chain
feature.description: A clean reference example with source evidence, traceability, verification criteria, linked test case, review state, safety classification, and cross-level consistency.

input_evidence.text: The rider shall be able to adjust display brightness manually between minimum and maximum brightness levels.
BrightnessController applies brightnessLevelPercent values in the allowed range from 0 to 100.

requirements.statement: The rider shall be able to manually adjust display brightness.
The system shall allow the rider to adjust display brightness manually from 0 percent to 100 percent.
requirements.verification_criteria: Set brightness to 0, 50, and 100 percent and verify that the display brightness changes accordingly.

requirements.statement: The brightness control software shall accept brightnessLevelPercent values from 0 to 100 and apply the selected value to the display brightness controller.
requirements.verification_criteria: Call the brightness control interface with 0, 50, and 100 percent and verify that the requested values are applied.

test_cases.title: Verify manual display brightness adjustment
test_cases.expected_result: The display brightness follows the selected manual brightness level from 0 percent to 100 percent.
